body: PASS 16 done, reporting back as asked. Couldn't independently confirm the raw Pre-Flight output myself -- the Journal log file is still frozen mid-buffer on my end (known MT4 issue, documented before), so I went on your description directly rather than keep blocking on seeing it myself. WARRANT#30 -> SLS(45)/9.59, compiled clean. What I built: ComputeLivingEdgeInversionTrim(), wired into the live conviction pipeline right after the two existing forge-4d trims. When a live signal fires in a session x displacement x volatility cell that's REAL-floor powered (n>=500) and its measured history significantly opposes the signal's own direction, conviction gets trimmed down (bounded 0.70-1.00, scales with how strong the opposition is). Deliberately did NOT flip any signal or invert any weight outright -- betting directly that an inversion is itself stable is exactly the trap the Living Edge thesis describes (it inverted, then inverted again). This is the safe, doctrine-correct response: aware of the contrary pattern, never betting on it being permanent. It's always-on, not gated behind a flag, because it can only ever reduce conviction -- same risk shape as the two trims that have been live since pass 9. One honest gap: I built this to be correct for whatever inversion pattern the scan finds, but I never actually saw the specific cells you're looking at, so once the Journal buffer flushes or a run finishes, worth cross-checking the printed inversion list against what this trim fires on. forge_ledger.json pass_16 filed. SLS(43) is still the live running build; SLS(44) and SLS(45) both queued, SLS(45) is the superset with everything so far.
